metadata.title:
DIRTY Bakeries DON'T


BOTHER CITY COUNCIL

Sanitary Inspection Ordinance Once More


Throttled in Committee.

pages.raw_text:
ments the proper and best place
for bakeries.


Bittrick retorted: There il not
a clean basement bakery In the
city. The bread is mixed on tables
near the windows. II the ordinance
is passed basement bakeries viii
practically be prohibited. The or.


dinance prohibits bakeries in base1


ments more than four feet deep.


Basement bakeries are dirty holes,


and nothing more. There Is no
ventilation and little light
Councilman Mohr said he Is get
ting fat on the bakery, wherefore


he is satisfied.
Ostrander said he thought the


ordinance merely a scheme Of the
health department to saddle the
expense of another inspector upon
the taxpayers.


Bittrick then asked why no


bakery proprietors were on hand
to oppose the ordinance. They


haven't the nerve to oppose Il
openly" he said. when inspec-
tors in a feeble endeavor to have
them clean up recommend some


changes the proprietors tell the
inspectors they have no author
| ity,"


Councilman Lambert said there


was a lot in the ordinance which


was foolish and he did not favor


recommending its passage.
Councilman Dalke seconded by
Councilman Watkins moved that
It be reported out. The motion waa


lost, Dalke and Watkins being tho


only two voices for it. When those
opposed were asked to signify their
vote several OF them grunted, and


Chairman Schiller took the grunts
for noes.
